title: Thrice Upon A Humanity The Deep Lore of NTE WIP
display_title: Thrice Upon A Humanity The Deep Lore of NTE WIP
wikitext:
[]: Thrice Upon A Humanity: The Deep Lore of NTE [WIP]             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Apr 24, 2020 5:36 pm I’ve been teasing a follow-up to the Adams/Children thread for a while, so let us begin! As before, this analysis is a result of work done mainly by myself and BlueBasilisk . Updates to this thread will come in between new installments of FBLM (see sig). Since the thread will take a while to really get revved up, here’s what you can eventually expect to be covered in detail... Proposition: The story of New Theatrical Edition occurs within a single, forward-moving timeline that encompasses thousands of years. Within this span of time, a similar series of events -- humans find gods, humans create Evas, Evas destroy Angels, artificial evolution is attempted via Impact -- has played out twice on Earth. The first “loop” involved a pre-Lilin civilization (or "PLC"), a technologically-advanced species of forerunners who wiped themselves out through First Impact. The second “loop” involves the PLC's successors, the Lilin, being guided to a state of advancement by seven surviving PLC representatives (Seele), with the ultimate intention of undoing the damage wrought by First Impact. The Final Impact, the last stage in this purification process, will produce a renewed third take on humanoid sapience for the planet Earth. ^ While extremely simplified, this gives you a general idea of how batshit insane this thread will eventually get. As they say in Japan, “Please look forward to it!” Table of Contents Part I. Introduction (Pact Between Man & God) A. Our Seele Is Different B. The Covenant C. Original Sin D. The Serpent E1 & E2. Divine Numbers (Background; Lilith's Resurrection & The True God) E3 & E4. Divine Numbers (Seven Motif; Seele = Seven Eyes) Part II. Signs of an Ancient World A. A Non-Human Intelligence B. Parallels to Nadia 's Atlantis 1. Mysterious Symbols Part III. Of Purification, Resurrection, and Deification -pending- I. Pact Between Man and God A. Not Your Mama's Global Conspiracy When NTE began, it was easy to assume that Seele wasn’t too different from the one we were used to. Sure, there was a different number of monoliths, they appeared only as monoliths from the start, and they now had a “covenant” with Lilith (whatever that meant), but they still seemed pretty much like your average doomsday cult of old men who secretly rule the world. Then Q dropped a bombshell on us: Eva 3.0 Dansubs wrote: GENDO: Though your souls may have taken on a different form, you are still products of the forbidden fruit. While it may be possible to live eternally, death must come to you as it does to us. In your quest to assist the evolution of those burdened with mortality, you gave us civilization. On behalf of all humanity, I express our gratitude. With your deaths, I will return your souls to where they belong.

[]: Re: Thrice Upon A Humanity: The Deep Lore of NTE             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Sat Apr 25, 2020 3:43 am Core in the Seele monoliths and Dummy System: Congratulations Reichu, once again you manage to drop a bombshell that was in plain sight this whole time

[]: Re: Thrice Upon A Humanity: The Deep Lore of NTE             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Apr 25, 2020 4:47 am I remember thinking it was suspicious that we never saw flesh and blood Seele members in Eva Jo and Ha like we did in NGE, and the monoliths straight up dying during Gendo’s monologue in Eva Q was a dead give away to me. But one of the things that kept me from making sense of anything beyond that was the fact that Seele wanted death, and were okay with dying at that moment despite the fact that they still have some sort of plan that’s unfolding. Why would Seele want to die when their plan (whatever it is) isn’t complete? What part of their plan makes sense to have Gendo to see it through, especially since, according to Kaji in Eva Ha, Seele wasn’t going to take the Godhood of Unit 01 lying down? That sounds like a huge breach of trust. Granted, it was immediately subverted by “Seele’s Boy” Kaworu, but still; that seems like a major breach of trust. I’m not picking holes in your theory or anything. I love the work you guys have done, and am excited to see where this idea (both in this thread and in the films) goes. I just had more questions at the end of that scene than I did before that scene as to Seele’s plan, since, you know, Seele’s dead now. These opened questions kinda irritate me, so I just try not to think about them that much, but with Thrice Upon a Time looming in the indefinite distance, it’s more difficult to ignore these questions. SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Thrice Upon A Humanity: The Deep Lore of NTE             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Sat Apr 25, 2020 12:50 pm It's-a-here!

[]: Re: Thrice Upon A Humanity: The Deep Lore of NTE             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Apr 26, 2020 1:55 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: I remember thinking it was suspicious that we never saw flesh and blood Seele members in Eva Jo and Ha like we did in NGE, and the monoliths straight up dying during Gendo’s monologue in Eva Q was a dead give away to me. But one of the things that kept me from making sense of anything beyond that was the fact that Seele wanted death, and were okay with dying at that moment despite the fact that they still have some sort of plan that’s unfolding. Why would Seele want to die when their plan (whatever it is) isn’t complete? What part of their plan makes sense to have Gendo to see it through, especially since, according to Kaji in Eva Ha, Seele wasn’t going to take the Godhood of Unit 01 lying down? That sounds like a huge breach of trust. Granted, it was immediately subverted by “Seele’s Boy” Kaworu, but still; that seems like a major breach of trust.

[]: Re: Thrice Upon A Humanity: The Deep Lore of NTE [WIP]             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 30, 2020 9:33 pm Who Are Seele, What Are Seele, and What Do They Want? To summarize from the previous post: 1) There are seven of them. 2) They predate human (= Lilin) civilization. 3) They, like humans (= Lilin), possess the Fruit of Knowledge. 4) They gave themselves unnaturally long life by preserving their souls inside monoliths that seem to utilize a form of core-based technology. 5) Their original biological forms seem to have been similar to ours (see: bones and soft tissues inside monoliths when they shut down). This isn’t all, of course. Let’s take a closer look. B. The Covenant Seele’s connection to Lilith has been enhanced significantly from what it was in NGE, where they gave lip service to piety but, ultimately, gods were just a means to an end for them. The first big reveal comes in 1.0, during Gendo’s otherwise unspectacular meeting with them following Eva-01’s first engagement: Eva Jo, Funi Subtitles wrote: SEELE 06: Annihilating the Angels is only part of our covenant with Lilith. KIEL: The Human Instrumentality Project. What we ultimately desire is the execution of that plan.

[]: Re: Thrice Upon A Humanity: The Deep Lore of NTE [WIP]             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri May 01, 2020 3:15 am Reichu wrote: Gendo and Fuyutsuki let us know that Seele plan to use Mark.06 to create this “true god” of theirs.

[]: Re: Thrice Upon A Humanity: The Deep Lore of NTE [WIP]             Quote Post by intermediateO » Fri May 01, 2020 9:04 am Just a quick question, cause I've never seen anyone adress it, even though it's been something in the back of my mind since 1.0. Has anyone adressed the theory of Classic Purple Mask Giant being Lilith actually, and Dogma Angel Mask Giant being Adam/an Adams? Just on a surface level, when I saw 1.0 originally, as a fan of the old series, it was kinda hard to not get to the final scene after enjoying all the updated designs and not go, "Wait. That's old Lilith. But. But new Lilith is in Dogma and all new and stuff." There's probably something really simple that can flush this theory down the pooper, but I do like the potential theming though, of both Misato and Kaworu being told the wrong giant is in the basement again.

[]: Re: Thrice Upon A Humanity: The Deep Lore of NTE [WIP]             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri May 01, 2020 9:57 am pwhodges wrote: Do you yet have any theory as to why this role passed from Mark06 to Eva13 ?

[]: Re: Thrice Upon A Humanity: The Deep Lore of NTE [WIP]             Quote Post by intermediateO » Fri May 01, 2020 10:44 am Reichu wrote: I'll be revisiting this tangle in an upcoming post, actually! It's a write-up I'm really looking forward to. A lot of strange things should hopefully click into place at that time.

[]: Re: Thrice Upon A Humanity: The Deep Lore of NTE [WIP]             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed May 06, 2020 3:09 am Picking up from last time... D. The Serpent The reason I bring all of this up is that the serpent in Seele’s symbol isn’t there simply to make them look sinister (though it does achieve that as well). Think about it… The Serpent, which is possessively coiled around the Fruit of Knowledge, is juxtaposed directly against the elements of Seele’s original logo. SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Thrice Upon A Humanity: The Deep Lore of NTE [WIP]             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Wed May 06, 2020 4:46 am Reichu wrote: This, naturally, raises a LOT of pretty weird questions about why this specific Angel is being used for this purpose — like, what’s its original “pre-Lilith” nature? Is there any way to know? (Yes, possibly, but I’ll hold onto that speculah for now.) Plus there’s the weirdness of the serpent form emerging from Mark.06 specifically. What the hell is that all about? How does my “the Angel is a mirror to Lilith” argument work in light of that?

[]: Re: Thrice Upon A Humanity: The Deep Lore of NTE [WIP]             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed May 06, 2020 6:12 am I wonder how much Eva Unit 01 actually represents some sort of “Eve.” I mean, it’s totally possible and kinda awesome, and it stays true to the Lilith/Eve dichotomy from certain versions of the Abrahamic Creation account. But, at the same time, in that scene in 2.22 Eva Unit 01 seems to have the color scheme of the Whore of Babylon as described in Revelation 17:4. And the woman was arrayed in purple and scarlet colour, and decked with gold and precious stones and pearls, having a golden cup in her hand full of abominations and filthiness of her fornication

[]: Re: Thrice Upon A Humanity: The Deep Lore of NTE [WIP]             Quote Post by Shun » Wed May 06, 2020 7:38 am Wow! These speculations are very detailed! Great! I hadn't tried to look details and references, but in my head I thought the link between the Seele, an ancient civilization and the core technology (monoliths), I had also thought about the new Seele logo. In particular I had notice this: In the Bible Eva falls into the snake trap and tastes the Fruit of knowledge. But since Lilith is present in Evangelion, and she is the divine entity associated with this FoK, I thought that she could be the element to consider for this role. Then: * Snake: Entity that causes betrayal -> Fruit of Knowledge -> Lilith * Apple: Fruit of Life -> Adam * Mask with 7 Eyes: God -> Infinity If the snake indicates a betrayal that occurred in ancient times, Seele could have played a role in this thing, for example by supporting the entity that carried out the betrayal, that is, Lilith. Perhaps in ancient times Adam (FoL) and Lilith (FoK) were a single ancestral entity (7 eyes) which then split, or there was an ancestral zero entity (7 eyes + FoL) and Lilith broke it ... the giant mouth, the Infinity ... maybe they are things related to First Impact. My speculation is: 1st: division of Infinity due to Lilith (betrayal); 2nd: approach between Adam and Lilith, division of Adam into the 4 Adams; Near3rd: approach between the soul of Lilith and Adams-01; 4th: approach between the body of Lilith, Adams-13 and Adams.06; Final: Adam + Lilith -> Infinity --> WIP Btw, the text of the Seele (Ode to Joy) is also interesting: Ueberm sternenzelt richtet gott wie wir gerichtet From above the firmament God judges as we have judged. WIP: World Instrumentality Project? I await the next posts! I hope I haven't spoiled something, otherwise I delete!

[]: Re: Thrice Upon A Humanity: The Deep Lore of NTE [WIP]             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed May 06, 2020 8:18 am Shun wrote: Wow! These speculations are very detailed! Great!

[]: Re: Thrice Upon A Humanity: The Deep Lore of NTE [WIP]             Quote Post by Shun » Wed May 06, 2020 9:54 am Ok, I'm glad I didn't spoiler anything, and I wasn't expecting to guess something ... I'm really curious, good work! WIP = Work In Progress I really thought it was a clue! Btw, WIP like World Instrumentality Project could be an idea if the final divinity (Infinity? Shin Evangelion?) were to have the power to change (Kaworu says 変える) and/or rebuild (Fuyutsuki says 作り直す) the world. Not necessarily the Earth, if the "world of harmony" were a spiritual version of the world, like Shambhala, it could be a nice idea. Unless the "world of harmony" is divinity himself.

[]: Re: Thrice Upon A Humanity: The Deep Lore of NTE [WIP]             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Thu May 07, 2020 2:46 am Reichu wrote: What do you mean by "symbolism"? The "serpent" that turns into an "apple" thing?

[]: Re: Thrice Upon A Humanity: The Deep Lore of NTE [WIP]             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu May 07, 2020 3:08 am Giji Shinka wrote: If the 12th angel represents the serpent/apple and is part of Lilith and her resurrection, then what about 10th angel? Does the whole Eva-01/10th angel/Rei - Lilith sequence have anything (similar) going on? Do you think it means anything else other than a sex metaphor?

[]: Re: Thrice Upon A Humanity: The Deep Lore of NTE [WIP]             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri May 15, 2020 3:25 am E. Divine Numbers (Especially Seven) 1. Background Seele's symbol  SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Thrice Upon A Humanity: The Deep Lore of NTE [WIP]             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri May 15, 2020 3:33 am (Splitting this in two to make it easier to link to specific parts later.) 3. Seven. Seven? Seven! So, to get through the rest of the material I had lined up for this section... The number “seven”, being of the Godly numbers and all, naturally has to show up a few more times. Lilith’s mask* has two holes for the eye sockets, and driven into each one is a bolt or a nail. (This explains why the unmasked Lilith in 3.0 has big bloody pits where eyes should be.) On the head of each bolt, we of course have the Seven Eyes symbol. But what’s this? Each bolt has a seven-sided head has as well! * (That’s what the CRC calls it, anyway. I feel like something weirder has to be going on, since this feature appears on Eva-01 when she merges with Lilith’s soul — implying it’s somehow intrinsic to Lilith — and it’s part of the anatomy of multiple Angels.) SPOILER: Show

Seven. Seven? Seven! So, to get through the rest of the material I had lined up for this section... The number “seven”, being of the Godly numbers and all, naturally has to show up a few more times. Lilith’s mask* has two holes for the eye sockets, and driven into each one is a bolt or a nail. (This explains why the unmasked Lilith in 3.0 has big bloody

Article: Thrice Upon A Humanity The Deep Lore of NTE WIP
 to show up a few more times. Lilith’s mask* has two holes for the eye sockets, and driven into each one is a bolt or a nail. (This explains why the unmasked Lilith in 3.0 has big bloody pits where eyes should be.) On the head of each bolt, we of course have the Seven Eyes symbol. But what’s this? Each bolt has a seven-sided head has as well! * (That’s what the CRC calls it, anyway. I feel like something weirder has to be going on, since this feature appears on Eva-01 when she merges with Lilith’s soul — implying it’s somehow intrinsic to Lilith — and it’s part of the anatomy of multiple Angels.) SPOILER: Show
